title: Creating App Engine handlers without requiring TLS is security-sensitive
description: App Engine handlers that do not require TLS (HTTPS) expose data in transit to interception and tampering. All sensitive endpoints should enforce TLS to protect confidentiality and integrity of user data and authentication tokens.
message: App Engine handler does not require TLS. Enforce HTTPS for all handlers.
impact: Unencrypted traffic can be intercepted, leading to data breaches and session hijacking.
recommendation: Configure all App Engine handlers to require secure (HTTPS) connections. Use 'secure: always' in handler definitions.

noncompliant.code:
handlers:
- url: /api/
  script: auto
  secure: optional
noncompliant.description: Handler allows both HTTP and HTTPS, exposing data to interception.

compliant.code:
handlers:
- url: /api/
  script: auto
  secure: always
compliant.description: Handler enforces HTTPS, protecting data in transit.

devsecops.security: TLS protects data in transit from eavesdropping and tampering.
devsecops.automation: CI/CD should enforce 'secure: always' for all handlers.
devsecops.compliance: TLS is required by PCI DSS, HIPAA, and other standards.

why_is_this_an_issue: Allowing HTTP exposes sensitive data to interception and manipulation by attackers.
how_to_fix: Set 'secure: always' for all App Engine handlers. Test endpoints to ensure HTTPS is enforced.

security_mappings.CWE: CWE-319: Cleartext Transmission of Sensitive Information
security_mappings.CERT: MSC12-C: Use SSL/TLS for sensitive communication
